# UC-17.1.65 · ISE Continuous Compliance Monitoring (CoCM) Grace-Period Tracking

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*If a computer becomes non-compliant, ISE gives it some time to fix itself. If many computers run out of time at the same moment, we ring the bell — often it's a fleet-wide problem, not bad behaviour.*

---

## Description

Detects CoCM grace-period expirations at scale — when many endpoints simultaneously hit grace expiry without remediation, indicating a fleet-wide compliance regression (broken agent, missing patch).

## Value

CoCM provides a window to remediate. When the grace expires fleet-wide, large numbers of endpoints are about to be quarantined; pre-emptive detection allows the platform team to extend grace temporarily and address root-cause.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Force grace expiry on >100 lab endpoints simultaneously. UC-17.1.65 fires.

### Negative scenario

Single grace expiry — does not fire.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=15091
| stats count by posture_policy
| where count > 100
```

## Visualization

Per-policy grace-expiry trend.

## Known False Positives

- Scheduled patch rollout window — expected expirations.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Continuous Compliance Monitoring](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_compliance_segmentation.html)
